About Us

A Robotics Club Born
from Passion

Moon Robotics (MoonBots VEX 862) is an Auckland-based VEX Robotics Club founded in 2024 by a father-son duo who have lived the VEX journey from the sidelines all the way to the world stage.

We serve students across Central Auckland, North Shore, East & West Auckland, offering free workshops, structured training programmes, and competitive team membership.

Our mission: make STEM education fun, accessible, and inspiring for every young learner aged 6–14, regardless of prior experience.

Our Story

From Parent Volunteer to
Club Founder & Head Referee

2000

The Journey Begins

Evan joins Lynfield College’s robotics club. Jason becomes a dedicated parent volunteer — driving to competitions, cheering from the sidelines, watching his son grow into a champion.

2020–2023

Evan’s Champion Journey

Evan competes at the highest levels, earning national and world championship titles across multiple seasons.

  • VEX IQ World Championship 2020–2021
  • VEX V5 World Championship 2021–2022
  • VEX V5 National Champion 2022
  • VEX V5 Engineering Challenge World Champion 2022
  • VEX V5 World Championship 2022–2023
2020–2026

Jason’s Volunteer & Referee Journey

Jason grows from sideline parent to a core pillar of the NZ VEX community.

  • VEX Volunteer 2020–2026
  • VEX Auckland Head Referee 2024–2026
  • Mentor & Coach, Lynfield College Robotics Club & Moon Robotics
  • VEX New Zealand Volunteer of the Year Award 2024
2024

Moon Robotics Founded

Father and son launch Moon Robotics (VEX 862), channeling years of competition and volunteer experience into building the next generation of Auckland engineers.

2024–2026

Dual Volunteer of the Year

Both Jason and Evan receive VEX New Zealand Volunteer of the Year awards in consecutive seasons.

🏉 VEX NZ Volunteer of the Year 2024-25 — Evan Huang
🏉 VEX NZ Volunteer of the Year 2025-26 — Jason Huang

Programs

How to Join

Four pathways from zero experience to competition-ready — including helping start clubs across NZ.

🎓

Free Workshop

Drop-in sessions at schools, libraries, and community events. No registration — just show up and experience robotics for free.

FREE
🔧

10-Session Training

Structured beginner programme covering VEX IQ building, mechanical design, and programming. For students aged 6–14 with no prior experience.

Enrol Now
🏆

Competition Team

Join a VEX team for the annual season. Design, build, and compete at Auckland regional events and the NZ National Championship.

Apply
🌎

Start a Local Club

Based outside Auckland? We help communities across New Zealand establish their own VEX Robotics clubs with mentorship and resources.

© 2024–2026 Moon Robotics

VEX Robotics

About VEX Robotics

VEX Robotics is the world’s largest and fastest-growing robotics competition, operating in 70+ countries and organised by the Robotics Education & Competition Foundation (RECF). It prepares students for real-world STEM careers through hands-on design, building, and programming challenges.

Moon Robotics offers two VEX programmes in Auckland:

VEX IQ — Ages 8–14. Snap-together plastic components. Focuses on teamwork, design process, and driver skills. Perfect for beginners.
VEX V5 — Ages 12+. Metal components, V5 smart motors, and advanced programming. For students ready to go deeper.
Annual season runs September through April, culminating in the VEX Robotics World Championship in Dallas, Texas.
Teams are judged on robot performance, engineering notebooks, and interview presentation.
Skills include: mechanical design, programming (Blocks / Python / C++), strategy, and teamwork.
70+

Competition Categories

VEX IQ

Ages 8–14. Plastic snap-together kits. Team & driver skills events. Focus on cooperation and fun.

VEX V5

Ages 12+. Metal components and smart electronics. Advanced programming and autonomous routines.

VEX U

VEX U

University level. Full engineering and programming complexity. Open design rules.

KIWIbots NZ

KIWIbots inspires and empowers
the next generation

KIWIbots is New Zealand’s official organiser for VEX IQ and V5 Robotics competitions, future-proofing Kiwi kids through STEM education and competitive robotics.

🔭 KIWIbots at a Glance

500+
Student competitors annually
50+
Teams nationwide
10+
Years running
NZ→World
World Championship pathway

🏆 Accolades

→
Multiple NZ teams have qualified for and competed at the VEX Robotics World Championship
→
NZ students recognised as world-class engineers and innovators on the global stage
→
Moon Robotics mentors awarded VEX NZ Volunteer of the Year 2024-25 and 2025-26

⚙️ How Does it Work?

Each season, KIWIbots announces a new game challenge. Teams design, build, and program their robots to compete in the challenge. The season progresses through scrimmages, regional qualifiers, and culminates at the NZ National Championship. Top teams earn the right to represent New Zealand at the VEX World Championship.

Competition Groups

VEX IQ

Elementary & Middle School. Team & driver skills. Ages 8–14.

VEX V5

High School. Advanced build & code. Ages 12–18.

VEX U

VEX U

University. Open design. Maximum engineering freedom.
